task: Implement the /login page per MoMorph/Figma design (fileKey 9ypp4enmFmdK3YAFJLIu6C, frame 662:14387 'Login'): SAA 2025 Root Further screen with Google-OAuth-only sign-in against the local Supabase project. Replace the prior email/password implementation.
mode: takumi interactive (MoMorph two-track parallel protocol; MoMorph MCP down, design via Figma MCP)
acceptanceCriteria: /login renders the SAA 2025 Root Further design: keyvisual background artwork, gradient overlays, semi-transparent header with SAA logo and static VN language selector, ROOT FURTHER logo, Vietnamese tagline, yellow LOGIN With Google button, bordered copyright footer

Clicking LOGIN With Google POSTs the loginWithGoogle server action and redirects to the local Supabase /auth/v1/authorize URL for provider google with a PKCE code_challenge and redirect_to /auth/callback
/auth/callback without a valid code redirects to /login?error=auth and the login page renders the Vietnamese error message Dang nhap that bai
Unauthenticated requests to protected paths redirect to /login while /login and /auth remain public with no redirect loop
pnpm build compiles with zero TypeScript errors and app code (app, lib, proxy.ts) lints with zero eslint errors
blastRadius: app/login/login-form.tsx deleted (obsolete email/password form) — nothing else imported it
login/LoginState exports removed from app/login/actions.ts — only the deleted form consumed them
lib/supabase/middleware.ts PUBLIC_PATHS now includes /auth — affects every request via proxy.ts matcher
contracts: UI-backend contract: google-login-button.tsx submits <form action={loginWithGoogle}> imported from app/login/actions.ts
OAuth callback contract: Supabase redirects to /auth/callback?code=...; route exchanges code for session then redirects to /; failure path is /login?error=auth
Supabase env contract: NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL + NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY from .env.local; Google provider config is user-owned in local Supabase (documented in .env.local.example)
